blurb: Funds and oversees B.C.'s health system — MSP coverage, hospitals and health authorities, PharmaCare, primary care, public health, and seniors' care.

goals.title: GOAL 1
goals.short_name: Every British Columbian has access to public health coverage and primary care
goals.description: The ministry enrols residents in public health and drug coverage, issues the BC Services Card, and connects people who don't have a family doctor to primary care.

main_services.name: Enrol in public health and drug coverage

supporting.name: Enrol in the Medical Services Plan (MSP)
supporting.desc: All B.C. residents must by law enrol in the Medical Services Plan, which pays for medically required services of physicians and surgeons, and dental or oral surgery performed in a hospital.
supporting.users: B.C. residents, new residents, people moving from another province

channels.label: BC Application for Health and Drug Coverage

legislation.full_name: Medicare Protection Act  -  Enrolment in the Medical Services Plan
Hospital Insurance Act  -  Insured hospital services

supporting.name: Apply for B.C. health and drug coverage using one form
supporting.desc: The B.C. Application for Health and Drug Coverage lets you apply for MSP, Fair PharmaCare and Supplementary Benefits at the same time, online or by mail.

channels.label: Application for BC Health and Drug Coverage (HLTH 101)

supporting.name: Get a BC Services Card
supporting.desc: Completing MSP enrolment at an ICBC driver licensing office produces a BC Services Card, which carries your Personal Health Number and provides access to provincial health care benefits.

channels.label: Book an ICBC driver licensing appointment

supporting.name: Register for the Fair PharmaCare plan
supporting.desc: Fair PharmaCare helps B.C. families pay for many prescription drugs, dispensing fees and some medical supplies, with coverage based on family income.

legislation.full_name: Pharmaceutical Services Act  -  PharmaCare plans and drug benefits

supporting.name: Apply for MSP Supplementary Benefits
supporting.desc: Supplementary Benefits provide partial payment for services such as acupuncture and massage therapy for individuals or families with an adjusted net income of $42,000 a year or less.
supporting.users: Lower-income B.C. residents enrolled in MSP

main_services.name: Find a family doctor or primary care

supporting.name: Register for the Health Connect Registry to find a family doctor or nurse practitioner
supporting.desc: People in B.C. who need a family doctor or nurse practitioner can register on the Health Connect Registry, which shares the list with attachment coordinators working to match people with primary care providers.
supporting.users: B.C. residents without a family doctor or nurse practitioner

supporting.name: Call HealthLink BC 8-1-1 for 24/7 health advice
supporting.desc: HealthLink BC connects British Columbians with a health service navigator any time of day or night, with access to registered nurses, dietitians, pharmacists and exercise professionals, in over 130 languages.

supporting.name: Get same-day care at an Urgent and Primary Care Centre
supporting.desc: Urgent and Primary Care Centres provide same-day, non-emergency urgent care and are often open evenings, weekends and statutory holidays, as an alternative to a hospital visit.
supporting.users: General public, people waiting for a family doctor

supporting.name: Access your health records through Health Gateway
supporting.desc: Health Gateway lets British Columbians view their health records, lab test results, medications and immunizations online.

goals.title: GOAL 2
goals.short_name: British Columbians get help paying for medications, medical supplies and care
goals.description: The ministry helps residents afford prescription drugs and medical supplies through PharmaCare, funds fertility treatment, and helps cover the cost of travel and accommodation for medical care.

main_services.name: Get help paying for prescription drugs and medical supplies

supporting.name: Get help paying for prescription drugs and medical supplies through BC PharmaCare
supporting.desc: BC PharmaCare is a public program that helps B.C. residents pay for many prescription drugs, some medical devices and supplies, and pharmacy services, processed at the pharmacy counter.

legislation.full_name: Pharmaceutical Services Act  -  PharmaCare plans and drug benefits

supporting.name: Ask your prescriber to apply for Special Authority drug coverage
supporting.desc: Some drugs need a Special Authority request from a prescriber before PharmaCare will cover them; requests must be approved before you pay for the prescription.
supporting.users: B.C. residents prescribed a restricted drug

supporting.name: Get a copy of your prescription records through PharmaNet
supporting.desc: British Columbians can get a record of the prescriptions filled at B.C. community pharmacies through Health Gateway or by ordering a copy through PharmaNet.

main_services.name: Get help with fertility treatment costs

supporting.name: Apply for publicly funded IVF treatment funding
supporting.desc: The Publicly Funded IVF Program provides one-time, income-based funding of up to $19,000 for a standard IVF cycle for eligible B.C. residents, applied for through a participating fertility clinic.
supporting.users: B.C. residents enrolled in MSP who need IVF to build their family

main_services.name: Get help with the cost of medical travel and accommodation

supporting.name: Apply for the Travel Assistance Program (TAP BC)
supporting.desc: TAP BC is a partnership between the Ministry of Health and private transportation carriers that offers discounted fares to residents travelling within B.C. for specialist medical care.
supporting.users: B.C. residents travelling for specialist medical care

supporting.name: Get accommodation help through the BC Family Residence Program
supporting.desc: The BC Family Residence Program provides accommodation and enhanced travel assistance so families can stay together when a child needs medical care at BC Children's Hospital or Sunny Hill Health Centre for Children.
supporting.users: Families of children needing hospital care away from home

supporting.name: Access regional medical travel assistance through Health Connections
supporting.desc: Health Connections is a regional medical travel assistance program run by four regional health authorities to help reduce transportation costs for rural residents travelling for care.
supporting.users: Rural and remote B.C. residents travelling for medical care

goals.title: GOAL 3
goals.short_name: British Columbians get mental health, substance use and public health protection
goals.description: The ministry supports access to mental health and substance use care, protects the public against infectious disease through immunization, and gives people a way to resolve concerns about health care they receive.

main_services.name: Get mental health and substance use support

supporting.name: Find mental health and substance use services near you
supporting.desc: HelpStartsHere lists over 2,500 mental health and substance use services and supports available across B.C.

channels.label: Mental health and substance use services

supporting.name: Call the Opioid Treatment Access Line for same-day addiction treatment
supporting.desc: The Opioid Treatment Access Line connects people to same-day opioid agonist treatment and a clinical team, free and confidential, every day from 9 am to 4 pm.

supporting.name: Access B.C.'s Road to Recovery addictions care model
supporting.desc: Road to Recovery is B.C.'s model for addictions care, tailoring treatment and recovery supports to each person's unique needs.
supporting.users: People seeking addictions treatment and their families

supporting.name: Get immunized against measles, flu, COVID-19 and other diseases
supporting.desc: Immunizations are available at community pharmacies, public health units, community health centres and primary care clinics throughout the province, following the B.C. Immunization Schedule.

legislation.full_name: Public Health Act  -  Communicable disease prevention and immunization

supporting.name: Get the HPV vaccine
supporting.desc: The HPV vaccine is offered free through B.C.'s school immunization program in grade 6, and free of charge to a wider range of eligible people up to age 45.

supporting.name: Submit or update your immunization records
supporting.desc: British Columbians can view immunization records on Health Gateway, or submit or update a record in the Provincial Immunization Registry if it is missing.

channels.label: Submit or update your immunization record

main_services.name: Resolve a concern about health care

supporting.name: File a complaint about the quality of health care services
supporting.desc: If a concern about the quality of health care you or a loved one received is not resolved where care was provided, you can file a formal complaint with the Patient Care Quality Office in your health region.

channels.label: Filing a Complaint About the Quality of Health Care Services

supporting.name: Request a review of extra billing for an MSP insured service
supporting.desc: If you think you were inappropriately charged for an MSP-insured service, you can request a review of the extra billing.

channels.label: Request a Review of Extra Billing

legislation.full_name: Medicare Protection Act  -  Prohibition on extra billing

supporting.name: File a complaint about an assisted living residence
supporting.desc: The Assisted Living Registry investigates complaints related to the health and safety of people living in assisted living residences.
supporting.users: Residents, families, staff and members of the public

channels.label: Making a Complaint About an Assisted Living Residence

legislation.full_name: Community Care and Assisted Living Act  -  Assisted living registry

supporting.name: File a complaint about a residential care facility
supporting.desc: If you have a concern about the health, safety or well-being of a person in a licensed residential care facility, you can file a complaint at any time.
supporting.users: Residents, families and members of the public

channels.label: Filing a Complaint About a Residential Care Facility

legislation.full_name: Community Care and Assisted Living Act  -  Residential care facility licensing

supporting.name: File a complaint about an emergency medical assistant
supporting.desc: The Emergency Medical Assistants Licensing Board investigates complaints regarding the practice of emergency medical assistants in British Columbia.

channels.label: Filing a Complaint About an Emergency Medical Assistant

legislation.full_name: Emergency Health Services Act  -  Emergency Medical Assistants Licensing Board

goals.title: GOAL 4
goals.short_name: British Columbians get supportive care as they age and access life event records
goals.description: The ministry helps people access subsidized home, community and residential care as their needs change, and the Vital Statistics Agency registers births, deaths and name changes for every British Columbian.

main_services.name: Get home, community or residential care

supporting.name: Apply for subsidized home and community care services
supporting.desc: Publicly subsidized home and community care services help people with acute, chronic, palliative or rehabilitative needs stay independent at home, or move into assisted living or long-term care when needed.
supporting.users: Seniors and people with ongoing health needs

legislation.full_name: Continuing Care Act  -  Home and community care services

supporting.name: Find assisted living or residential care
supporting.desc: Understand the difference between assisted living residences and licensed residential care facilities, and find options through the Assisted Living Registry.
supporting.users: Seniors and people needing daily living support

legislation.full_name: Community Care and Assisted Living Act  -  Assisted living registry

main_services.name: Register a birth, death, marriage or name change

supporting.name: Register your newborn's birth
supporting.desc: Online Birth Registration lets you register your child's birth and order a birth certificate at the same time; registration is free and takes 3-4 weeks to process.

legislation.full_name: Vital Statistics Act  -  Registration of births

supporting.name: Order a birth, death or marriage certificate
supporting.desc: The Vital Statistics Agency's online certificate ordering system lets British Columbians order certificates, certified registration copies and extracts, or apply in person at a Service BC location.

legislation.full_name: Vital Statistics Act  -  Certificates and certified copies

supporting.name: Apply for a legal change of name
supporting.desc: British Columbians can apply to legally change their name through the Vital Statistics Agency.

legislation.full_name: Vital Statistics Act  -  Change of name

supporting.name: Register a death
supporting.desc: Learn what to do when someone dies in B.C., including registering the death with the Vital Statistics Agency.

legislation.full_name: Vital Statistics Act  -  Registration of deaths

relationships.service: Report a death to the BC Coroners Service

foundational_services.name: Health system funding and payment to physicians and health authorities
foundational_services.desc: Administers MSP fee-for-service payments to physicians, funding agreements with B.C.'s health authorities, and hospital funding under the Hospital Insurance Act.
foundational_services.users: Internal program staff, health authorities, physicians

foundational_services.name: Provincial public health surveillance and health officer guidance
foundational_services.desc: The Office of the Provincial Health Officer monitors population health, issues public health orders, and provides medical health officer guidance to protect British Columbians.

foundational_services.name: Health profession college oversight and credential standards
foundational_services.desc: Oversight of the health profession regulatory colleges under the Health Professions and Occupations Act, setting standards for professional credentialing and public protection.
